Details: Two studies suggest that ultraviolet irradiation from natural sunlight combined with a catalyst can effectively degradate oxamyl [1515, 1517]. The catalysts tested were titanium dioxide (TiO2) and sodium decatungstate (Na4W10O32). One study showed oxamyl degradation of 80% with 0.2 g/L TiO2 and 85% with 1.5E-3 mol/L Na4W10O32 [1515]. The other study showed approximately 84% removal in 65 minutes with 0.2 g/L TiO2 [1517].
